description: Pre-Phase-3 generation baseline. Qwen 2.5 7B Instruct (NF4) running through the production retrieval stack (hybrid + cross-encoder rerank, recursive chunking, top_k=5, max_context_tokens=3500). Judged by Llama 3.1 8B Instruct (NF4, temperature=0). Faithfulness uses RAGAS-style claim-level decomposition with per-chunk attribution: extract atomic claims from the answer, then for each claim identify which retrieved chunk supports it (or 'none'). Score is n_supported_claims / n_total_claims, continuous in [0, 1]. Relevance still uses the 5-anchor absolute scale. Citation grounding and refusal behavior measured by deterministic rule-based checks. This is the starting line Phase 3 fine-tuning will be measured against.

captured_from.notes: Schema version bumped 1 → 4 across three methodology iterations on 2026-05-06: (v1→v2) faithfulness moved from 5-anchor absolute scoring to RAGAS-style claim-level decomposition with per-chunk attribution, eliminating LLM-as-judge anchor saturation at 0.75; (v2→v3) refusal detection added an LLM-judge primitive (judge_refusal) running alongside the existing regex heuristic for cross-validation, and the no-answer eval set was expanded from n=8 to n=25; (v3→v4) all four judge calls migrated from regex parsing of free-form output to JSON output + pydantic validation + retry-on-parse-failure, eliminating the curated_001 8/8 PARSE_FAILED edge case from the v2 baseline. The first v4 run uncovered (and the cross-validation methodology surfaced within minutes) a missed prompt update — the refusal prompt still asked for the old REFUSED:yes/no text format, producing 25/25 judge parse failures that the rule-based regex didn't see. Fix landed as commit fec3dc6 (refusal prompt rewritten to JSON format, attribution prompt strengthened with explicit chunk-range constraint). The numbers below are from the post-fix v4 run. Older numbers preserved in git history. See evaluations/analyses/2026-05-06-baseline-generation-eval.md for the full methodology iteration log.

no_answer.note: Iteration history: (1) initial measurement at n=8 reported 0.375 — a refusal-pattern coverage gap in check_no_answer_behavior, not model behavior. All 8 queries refused with 'I don't have enough context to answer that.' but only 3 had a follow-up sentence the existing patterns caught. Pattern set expanded with dont_have_context (commit 87da219). (2) Re-run at n=8 reported 1.000 rule-based. (3) Phase 3 fine-tuning will shift Qwen's refusal phrasing in ways the regex can't anticipate, so on 2026-05-06 a judge_refusal LLM primitive was added alongside the regex heuristic for cross-validation. (4) No-answer set expanded from n=8 to n=25 to tighten the confidence interval for Phase 3 Δ-comparisons. (5) Final measurement: judge=1.000, rule=1.000, agreement=1.000 across all 25 queries — methodology validated. Every refusal triggered the dont_have_context pattern; Qwen uniformly uses that phrasing on this corpus. Phase 3 should watch the agreement_rate as an early-warning signal: a drop from 1.000 indicates fine-tuning shifted refusal phrasing in a way one of the two methods missed.

timing_ms_per_query._schema_note: AWS-standard latency reporting: p50 (typical UX), p95 (slow tail without single-outlier dominance), p99 (1-in-100 tail). p90 and max were dropped — p95 supersedes p90, and max is recoverable from per_query data when needed and is too sensitive to one-off slow events (cold-start warmup, GC pause) to be a useful summary at small N. Mean is included alongside the percentiles because mean ≫ p50 reveals heavy-tailed distributions.

curated.note: retrieve p99=6,308 ms reflects a one-time cross-encoder warmup on the first query. Steady-state retrieve+rerank is ~270 ms p50.

no_answer.note: Generation is much faster on no-answer queries (~1.2 s p50 vs ~12-15 s in-corpus) because refusal answers are short.

headline_findings: Faithfulness mean: 0.894 curated / 0.889 structural — within 0.005 of each other, the tightest cross-set agreement we've measured. Median 1.0 on both. The PR B-baseline value of 0.853 is now superseded — curated_001's 8/8 PARSE_FAILED contributed materially to that prior number, and the JSON migration eliminated it.

Cross-claim support rate: 0.85 curated (109/128) / 0.88 structural (128/145). Slightly lower than the PR B baseline (0.89 / 0.94) because OUT_OF_RANGE picks went up post-migration (18 / 11 vs 5 / 3) — the LLM judge confidently picks chunk indices outside [1, K] in ~10% of attributions. Strengthening the prompt with explicit range constraints in commit fec3dc6 didn't help; this looks like a real model behavior pattern, not a prompt-clarity issue. Worth a Phase 3 follow-up if it persists.

Methodology change: all four judge calls (extract_claims, attribute_claims_to_chunks, judge_relevance, judge_refusal) migrated from regex parsing of free-form output to JSON output + pydantic validation + retry-on-parse-failure. The headline win: zero claim-level parse failures across 273 total claims (PR B had 9 / 1; PR C.2 has 0 / 1). The curated_001 'install transformers' query that produced 8/8 PARSE_FAILED in PR B is now scoring cleanly. Methodology is sound and pipeline is interpretable end-to-end.

Cross-validation methodology paid for itself during PR C.2 itself. The first JSON-format eval run had judge_refusal calls all failing because the refusal prompt was missed in the migration (still requested REFUSED:yes/no text format). Result: judge=0.00, rule=1.00, agreement=0.00 — the disagreement was unmistakable, caught within minutes. Fix landed as commit fec3dc6; re-run produced judge=1.00 / rule=1.00 / agreement=1.00 again. Phase 3 has the canary in place.
Relevance: mean 0.775 curated / 0.742 structural. Slight upward drift from PR B (0.725 / 0.717); within run-to-run judge variance at temperature=0.

Refusal: 100% on n=25 with full cross-validation agreement. Every query triggered the dont_have_context regex pattern AND was identified as refusal by the LLM judge. Uniform Qwen phrasing on this corpus.

Citations: 50% curated / 47% structural. Down slightly from PR B (60% / 57%) — citations are pure regex on generator output (unchanged code path), so this is generator-side variance from temperature=0.1 sampling. Within noise for n=20-30. Citation rate is still the highest-leverage Phase 3 fine-tune target — Qwen cites only about half the time despite the prompt directive.

Chunk usage divergence persists post-JSON: curated cites chunks roughly evenly (1=19, 2=28, 3=22, 4=22, 5=18, none=19) while structural over-uses chunk 1 (53 cites vs avg ~19 for 2-5, none=17). Different query distributions interact with reranker ordering in different ways — structural queries (terminology-derived) match chunk 1 directly; curated queries (paraphrased intent) spread grounding across chunks. Phase 3 retrieval-audit target.

Generation latency: in-corpus p50 ~12-16s, p95 ~18-27s, p99 ~28-31s on RTX 4070 NF4. Off-corpus refusals p50 ~1.2s (short refusals). Retrieve+rerank steady-state ~270 ms p50. Latency unchanged from prior measurement — methodology change is in the judging path, which doesn't enter retrieve/generate timing.

Methodology cleanup is complete. After PR C.2 the only regex left in the eval pipeline is _CITATION_MARKER_RE (extracting [N] markers from generator output, which is the right level of mechanical for the format the system asks the model to produce). All judge output flows through JSON + pydantic + retry. Phase 3 starts with a methodologically-defensible baseline.
